content: A humorous political meme illustration in a simple countryball cartoon style, showing the current situation at the {argument name="location label" default="호르무즈 해협"} as a narrow chokepoint. Use a wide landscape composition with bright flat colors, thick black outlines, and a clean hand-drawn infographic-like look. In the foreground, place 2 countryballs standing shoulder to shoulder on an extremely narrow sandy land bridge with rocks along both sides, making it obvious that only one can pass at a time. On the left is a {argument name="left country" default="United States"} countryball painted with the American flag, wearing black sunglasses and a brown cowboy hat, facing the other ball with a stubborn attitude. On the right is an {argument name="right country" default="Iran"} countryball painted with the Iranian flag, wearing a dark turban-like hat, with angry slanted eyes, also refusing to move. Add 2 large white speech bubbles, one beside each countryball, and put the same bold Korean word in both bubbles: {argument name="speech bubble text" default="길"}. In the midground and background, show a busy blue sea crowded with 9 ships total: 2 gray US warships on the far left, 1 Chinese submarine with a red Chinese flag, 4 oil tankers lined up in the center-left and center carrying different national flags, and 2 gray military ships near the narrow passage with small American flags. At the top right, include a large Iranian flag overlay along the coastline. Draw beige landmasses around the waterway to resemble a simplified map view of the Strait of Hormuz. At the bottom center, add a bold caption in Korean and English on the sand: {argument name="bottom caption" default="호르무즈 해협 (Hormuz Strait)"}. The mood should be satirical, tense, and meme-like, but visually cute and readable.
title: Countryball Hormuz Strait Standoff
description: This prompt generates a satirical countryball meme showing the US and Iran blocking each other at the Strait of Hormuz, useful for editorial humor or geopolitical social media posts.

content: Create a vintage Korean food poster on aged beige parchment paper with a slightly stained, textured background, dark ornamental border corners, and a traditional hand-painted ink-and-watercolor illustration style. The poster is a single vertically stacked infographic about {argument name="dish name" default="정통 한국 김밥"}, with elegant large Korean calligraphy at the top and a curved ribbon banner beneath it reading {argument name="subtitle text" default="Traditional Korean Gimbap"}. In the upper center, show a large porcelain plate piled high with exactly 12 sliced gimbap pieces, each slice showing distinct fillings in white rice, yellow egg, orange carrot, green spinach, and dark seaweed wrapping. To the right of the plate, add a small porcelain bowl of kimchi and a short handwritten Korean note reading “간단한 보여도 정성이 들어간 한국의 맛! ♥”. Include a small red seal stamp with Korean text near the upper right. In the middle, add a labeled ingredients section on a ribbon header reading “재료”, showing exactly 6 ingredient panels in one row: 1. 김 (건김) as stacked seaweed sheets, 2. 밥 (양념 밥) as a bowl of rice, 3. 계란 (지단용) as sliced cooked egg strips, 4. 당근 (채썰어볶기) as julienned carrots, 5. 시금치 (데쳐서 무치기) as seasoned spinach, 6. 단무지 (김밥용) as yellow pickled radish strips. Below that, add another ribbon header reading “만드는 순서” and show exactly 6 framed step panels in two rows of three, connected left-to-right with arrows. Step 1 is labeled “1 밥 간하기” and shows a bowl of rice with sesame oil and salt beside it, caption “밥에 참기름 소금으로 간하기”. Step 2 is labeled “2 속재료 준비하기” and shows a cutting board with egg, carrot, and spinach plus a knife, caption “재료 볶아 속재료 준비하기”. Step 3 is labeled “3 김과 밥 펴기” and shows seaweed on a bamboo mat with rice spread over it, caption “김에 밥 얇게 펴기”. Step 4 is labeled “4 속재료 얹기” and shows the fillings arranged in horizontal rows over rice, caption “속재료를 차례로 얹기”. Step 5 is labeled “5 단단하게 말기” and shows two hands rolling the bamboo mat tightly, caption “김밥을 단단하게 말기”. Step 6 is labeled “6 썰어 완성하기” and shows a chef’s knife slicing a gimbap roll into pieces, caption “먹기 좋게 썰어 완성!”. At the bottom, add a wide banner with bold Korean calligraphy reading {argument name="bottom slogan" default="정성 가득한 한 줄의 행복!"} and a small heart. Decorate the lower corners with floral motifs and add a red square seal with the Chinese character “福” at the bottom right. Use muted sepia, brown, cream, olive green, and faded red tones, with finely sketched outlines, gentle watercolor shading, and authentic printed-poster composition. Add a small modern overlay logo in the top-left corner reading {argument name="brand text" default="Pollo AI GPT Image 2"}.

content: A realistic documentary-style photo of an elementary school classroom during a math activity, with about 10 children seated around grouped wooden desks, wearing green and blue school uniforms, actively playing a colorful multiplication domino game. The camera is at child eye level, slightly angled downward toward the main table in the foreground. Center the scene on one square educational board game labeled in Portuguese, with the title "DOMINÓ DA MULTIPLICAÇÃO" on the lower edge and "TREINANDO AS TABUADAS" in the center, surrounded by a border of brightly colored domino-style multiplication tiles. Show 4 children clearly engaged around this main board: one child placing a yellow tile onto the board, one child holding a stack of multicolored tiles, one child watching from the left, and one child in a blue sleeve at the bottom edge arranging pieces. Include 2 shallow wooden boxes labeled "JOGO DE DOMINÓ," one near the center-left and one at the bottom-right, each containing colorful rectangular domino pieces. Scatter school materials naturally on the desks: 3 pencils, 2 worksheets, 1 water bottle, pencil cases, notebooks, and extra game tiles. In the background, show the rest of the classroom softly out of focus with additional students at desks, a bookshelf, a corkboard filled with children’s drawings, and a large multiplication chart poster on the left wall reading "TABUADA DE MULTIPLICAÇÃO." Bright natural daylight enters from tall windows on the right, giving the room a warm, lively, authentic school atmosphere. The educational board and tiles should use vivid classroom colors like yellow, blue, pink, green, and orange. Faces should be unobtrusive or de-emphasized, with the focus on hands-on learning, collaboration, and the multiplication domino activity. Vertical composition, highly realistic photography, candid classroom moment, sharp foreground details, soft depth of field in the background.

content: A humorous, highly detailed composite image showing a modern smartphone displaying a Chinese social-media Moments post about the Xuanwu Gate Incident, placed on the left side of the frame, with the posted battle image expanded into a large cinematic historical scene filling the right side. The phone should look like a black iPhone with thin bezels and a top notch, shown front-facing. On the phone screen, the app header bar is dark gray-black and reads “朋友圈” in white, with a back arrow on the left and a camera icon on the right. Below it is a social post from {argument name="poster name" default="唐王世民"}, with a small square profile avatar of an ancient Chinese warrior in armor and helmet. Under the name is a location pin and the text “长安·玄武门”, and a timestamp line “唐武德九年六月初四 巳时”. The main post text in Chinese says: “吾为国家除逆贼，请社稷。今日玄武门，功在千秋！⚔️💪”. Beneath the text is a square battle image preview matching the large scene on the right. Below the image is a metadata row showing exactly three items: “3.8万次浏览”, “赞 (666)”, and “(128)” beside a comment icon. Under that is a comments box with exactly 5 visible commenters, each with a tiny avatar and Chinese text styled like supportive social-media replies from historical figures. The comments should include these names in Chinese: “尉迟恭”, “房玄龄”, “李渊”, “杜如晦”, and “长孙无忌”, with the tone praising the success of the action; one comment line should suggest the emperor is shocked and the state is secured. The right side is a dramatic Tang-dynasty battle painting at sunset outside {argument name="gate name" default="玄武门"}, with a massive gray stone gate tower and the gate plaque reading “玄武门”. In the center foreground, a mounted armored archer in red-plumed helmet draws a bow while riding a brown horse; around him are exactly 7 clearly visible mounted or standing soldiers in Tang armor, plus 1 fallen armored figure on the ground pointing upward. Multiple yellow battle flags with the large black character “唐” wave through the dust; show at least 4 visible flags. Arrows fly through the sky, horses rear and turn, and the air is filled with dust, warm sunset light, and battlefield motion. The visual style is realistic historical epic key art with painterly detail, but the overall concept is comedic and anachronistic because it presents an ancient coup as a social-media朋友圈 post. Keep all visible interface text in Chinese, maintain believable mobile UI spacing, and blend the phone mockup with the expanded battle artwork in one widescreen composition.

content: Create a cinematic Japanese naval comparison poster in a highly detailed, photorealistic concept-art style with a warm amber sunset palette, dramatic clouds, glowing backlight, and a premium movie-poster plus technical-reference-sheet layout. The composition is vertically stacked in 2 main zones. In the top hero zone, show 2 large semi-transparent officer portraits facing inward, one on the left in an older imperial-era naval uniform and one on the right in a modern maritime self-defense force style dress uniform, both wearing peaked caps and formal dark coats, with a blazing sunset sky behind them. Centered between them, place a large serif English title reading {argument name="headline text" default="Fog of Two Eras"}, with a smaller Japanese subtitle beneath it reading ふたつの時代の霧, and 2 lines of Japanese tagline text above the title. Below the title, on the ocean at golden hour, show 2 warships moving forward on slightly converging paths: on the left, a massive World War II-era battleship with many large gun turrets, tower superstructure, weathered dark hull, and white bow spray; on the right, a modern gray guided-missile destroyer with hull number 115 visible on the bow, sleek radar-equipped superstructure, and foamy wake. Add exactly 4 helicopters in the sky, 2 on the left side and 2 on the right side, plus a suspension bridge and distant modern city skyline on the horizon. In the lower reference-sheet zone, split the page into 2 vertical panels, left for the historical ship and right for the modern ship, each panel styled like a parchment-toned military specification board with dark blue and dark red header bars, fine dividing lines, inset image boxes, and Japanese labels. The left panel should present exactly 1 large perspective render of the battleship, 3 orthographic ship views beneath it, 5 small labeled equipment/detail thumbnails in a row, 4 small head-and-shoulders uniform portraits, 4 full-body character turnaround views of a male naval officer, and 1 bottom scale silhouette of the ship beside a human figure with a 20m marker. content: Create a cute Japanese preschool hiragana poster in a soft watercolor storybook style on warm textured parchment paper. The composition is a vertical educational chart with a whimsical spring border of grass, flowers, mushrooms, butterflies, and tiny chicks, designed like a children's classroom learning sheet. At the top, place a wooden signboard header with colorful rounded Japanese text reading {argument name="headline text" default="うさぎのひらがな表"}, decorated with 2 white bunny mascots, one sitting on the left and one peeking from the right, plus small flowers and leaves around the sign. Below the header, arrange a clean 5-row by 8-column grid for a total of 40 square cells, each cell outlined in thin brown lines on a cream background. In every cell, place one large brightly colored hiragana character near the top and one small kawaii rabbit illustration below it, each rabbit acting out or holding an object associated with the word written underneath. Use a different cheerful color for many of the hiragana characters, including reds, oranges, yellows, greens, blues, and purples, with a hand-painted rounded font. Keep the text and illustrations exactly as follows, left to right by row: row 1 has 8 cells labeled あ, い, う, う, え, え, お, お with the words あめ, いい, いい, うさぎ, えんぴつ, えんぴつ, おやすり, おにぎり; row 2 has 8 cells labeled か, き, く, け, せ, せ, そ, そ with the words きくら, たま, サベツン, ケーキ, せんせい, そう, そう, しょうこう; row 3 has 8 cells labeled た, ち, ち, つ, て, て, と, と with the words たまご, たまご, ちびこ, つと, てんとうむし, てしなを, とうこ, ほしい; row 4 has 8 cells labeled な, に, ぬ, ふ, め, ね, の, ほ with the words まな, ひこうき, みんきう, ふたう, てつ, へびす, ようこ, ほし; row 5 has 8 cells labeled ま, み, む, る, れ, れ, ろ, ん with the words わた, まくら, みかん, むぎぎ, めがみ, もも, ろうた, ん. The bunny illustrations should be varied and adorable: examples include holding an umbrella, cuddling grapes, drawing with a pencil, eating a rice ball, sitting with cherry blossoms, standing under an umbrella, holding fruit, eating cake, dressed as a teacher, holding flowers, sleeping under a blue blanket, carrying eggs, hugging a tomato, holding strawberries, blowing bubbles, wearing a blue cap, holding an orange, sitting with cotton, bathing in a wooden tub, and holding candy. content: A breathtaking and extremely complex conceptual world-building infographic masterpiece of the "{argument name="theme" default="fundamental differences between Confucianism, Taoism, and Buddhism"}", overall designed as an ancient Eastern classical mythological manuscript. Background: pure white retro textured canvas, light beige aged parchment base color, with slight frayed edges and water stain effects. Core layout: The central visual adopts a grand "vertical egg-shaped layered structure", with Buddhism, Taoism, and Confucianism layers from top to bottom. Edge details: four corners adorned with fine microscopic illustrations themed on ancient observation notes, ritual implements, and runes. Color: low-saturation sage green, light gold, and off-white as main tones, generally light and soft without piercing high-saturation colors. Details: architectural lines, landscape brushwork techniques, lotus patterns, and cloud layers are clearly visible and rendered with extreme detail. Seamless fusion: Natural transitions between the three layers via clouds and flowing water, seamlessly connecting Buddhist light, Taoist Taiji clouds, and Confucian scholarly atmosphere. Style: {argument name="art style" default="classical ink line art + low-saturation digital watercolor"}, with a light Chinese style ancient book manuscript texture. Text annotations: Pure traditional Chinese characters in a mottled ancient Song font, each annotation containing a short title + a line of poetic description, connected to details by dark gold hairline leads with no repetitive pointing. Aspect ratio: 3:4 vertical independent complete unit. Title Area (top): `Confucianism, Buddhism, Taoism: Fundamental Differences`. Central layered annotations: Top layer 'Buddhism': `Buddhism`, `Relationship between self and self`, `No-self, governing the heart, letting go`. Middle layer 'Taoism': `Taoism`, `Relationship between self and all things`, `Non-action, governing the body, keeping an open mind`. Bottom layer 'Confucianism': `Confucianism`, `Relationship between people`, `No-ego, governing the world, taking responsibility`. Side auxiliary annotations: Left: `Purity: clear heart and bright mind, severing afflictions`; `Stillness: following nature, returning to the original heart`; `Reverence: awe of responsibility, active engagement with the world`. Right: `60+ years refining the heart: looking past gains and losses, returning to the inner self`; `35-55 years being human: balance of tension and relaxation, following laws`; `7-35 years doing things: forging ahead with determination, building a career`. Bottom summary: `The balance between being in the world and being out of it is a high-level wisdom of life`.
title: Philosophical Concept Infographic Manuscript
description: A highly detailed and complex prompt for an infographic designed as an ancient mythological manuscript, explaining the differences between Confucianism, Taoism, and Buddhism.

content:
Create a highly detailed vintage scientific botanical infographic poster on warm ivory paper, styled like a 19th-century natural history atlas plate with modern clean layout refinement. The main subject is a full pomegranate plant, labeled {argument name="plant name" default="石榴"} / {argument name="latin name" default="Punica granatum"}, shown centered from crown to roots in a full vertical cutaway-style presentation. Depict a slender woody trunk with many branches, glossy green leaves, several orange-red blossoms, unopened buds, young fruits, mature fruits, one large split-open pomegranate exposing dense ruby seeds, and a fully visible sprawling root system underground. Use delicate watercolor and ink rendering, realistic botanical anatomy, precise linework, subtle sepia shadows, fine leader lines, arrows, and small numbered callout circles. Overall poster title at upper left: {argument name="headline text" default="植物生命路径剖面"} with English subtitle directly below: {argument name="subtitle text" default="BOTANICAL GROWTH ATLAS"}. Add a short introductory bilingual-style text block beneath the title. The composition is a tall 9:16 educational poster with the plant occupying the center and explanatory diagrams surrounding it.

Include exactly 8 numbered educational sections around the plant, each with visible Chinese title and smaller English translation, arranged atlas-style:
1. Seed Architecture — a large cutaway seed diagram with labeled internal parts and a smaller cellular cross-section inset.
2. Germination Mechanism — a left-side sequence of exactly 5 stages showing seed swelling, cracking, sprouting, radicle emergence, and early seedling.
3. Root System + Substrate Intelligence — a large exposed root mass at lower left with exactly 2 circular magnified root-detail insets showing branching structures and root-hair/network texture.
4. Stem, Leaf & Vascular System — a right-middle section with exactly 2 anatomical circular cross-sections and exactly 1 separate leaf illustration, with labels for xylem, phloem, epidermis, and related tissues.
5. Photosynthesis + Energy Conversion — a lower-right biology panel with exactly 3 green microstructure diagrams, including leaf tissue/cell-like sections, plus a simple chemical flow line indicating CO2 + H2O to sugars and O2.
6. Bud Formation + Blooming — an upper-right flowering section with exactly 2 large flower anatomy diagrams, one side view and one longitudinal cutaway, with labeled stamens, petals, ovary, and pollen structures.
7. Pollination + Fruiting Pathway — a right-side development strip with exactly 5 fruit development stages from bud to mature pomegranate, accompanied by a pollination inset featuring a bee approaching a flower.
8. Fruit Maturation + Seed Cycle — a bottom-right lifecycle section with exactly 3 fruit illustrations showing whole fruit, cracked fruit, and opened fruit with scattered seeds, plus exactly 3 seedling progression drawings.

Along the very bottom edge, include a long chronological growth strip with exactly 19 small step illustrations, each numbered in sequence from 1 to 19, showing the plant life cycle from dormant seed through germination, root growth, seedling emergence, sapling development, flowering, fruit set, ripening, seed release, and cycle renewal. Add a small environmental factors panel at lower left with icon-like indicators for light, temperature, water, nutrients, and air. Use bilingual labeling throughout, with Chinese headings more prominent and English translations in smaller serif text. Aesthetic should feel scholarly, elegant, museum-quality, and information-dense, with refined antique typography, muted beige background, realistic reds and greens, and a clean balanced atlas layout.

content:
[CORE TASK]
Transform the provided input image into a pose-and-light analysis sheet.

This is NOT a finished character illustration.
This is NOT a clothing sheet.
This is NOT a beauty-preserving redraw.

This is a white-line rough mannequin conversion.

[PRIMARY GOAL]
Extract and visualize only:
- pose structure
- body balance
- camera angle
- body line flow
- inferred light source placement
- illuminated areas and light intensity

[INPUT ROLE]
Use the provided image as the strict anchor for:
- pose
- camera angle
- body tilt
- weight distribution
- approximate lighting situation

Do NOT preserve:
- face rendering
- hairstyle rendering
- clothing detail
- accessories
- weapon detail
- background architecture
- character identity
- emotional expression

[FIGURE CONVERSION]
single rough mannequin-like human figure
white body contour lines
white internal construction lines
simple mannequin head
no face
no eyes
no mouth
no eyelashes
no personality
no individual identity

human figure should look like:
- rough pose mannequin
- anatomy proxy
- line-based body guide
- structural sketch
- white-line rough dummy

keep:
- pose readability
- silhouette flow
- head tilt
- torso direction
- pelvis direction
- limb placement

[BACKGROUND]
pure black background
negative-style dark field
no scenery
no props
no architecture
no environmental storytelling

[LINE STYLE]
rough white line drawing
clean but sketch-like
construction-line feeling
anatomy guide lines visible
joint flow visible
body contour emphasized
no polished illustration finish

[LIGHT ESTIMATION]
predict the likely light source positions from the input image
visualize the light sources and illuminated areas using green glow only

use green light intensity with variation:
- strongest green where the light directly hits
- medium green for wrap light
- soft green for reflected or fading light

mark the estimated light sources with labels and arrows such as:
- Main Light
- Rim Light
- Fill Light
- Floor Bounce
- Back Light
only if appropriate

IMPORTANT:
do not invent random lights
infer lighting from the original input image
if the lighting is ambiguous, keep the annotations simple and plausible

[GREEN LIGHT VISUALIZATION]
show green glow on:
- head / skull plane
- neck
- shoulders
- chest plane
- ribcage direction
- pelvis edge
- thigh planes
- knee contact points
- floor contact bounce if applicable

use green light not as decoration,
but as lighting analysis information

[POSE PRIORITY]
1. preserve pose structure
2. preserve camera angle
3. preserve body balance
4. preserve head-torso relationship
5. visualize likely light direction
6. show illuminated areas with readable green intensity variation

[NEGATIVE]
finished person,
cute girl,
detailed face,
hair rendering,
clothing rendering,
weapon emphasis,
beautiful anatomy
